title: HTTP Client Reuse, Retrieval, Redirects, Validators, and Bounded Bodies · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for http client reuse, retrieval, redirects, validators, and bounded bodies and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, network, DNS, parser, filesystem, privacy, accessibility, legal-review, scale, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded HTTP page-fetch contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Reuse one configured client or bounded pool and close it deliberately so connection, proxy, TLS, cookie, and resource ownership are visible.
Use safe retrieval methods and an honest User-Agent, Accept, Accept-Encoding, conditional, and contact policy without impersonation.
Classify transport, status, media type, length, content coding, and domain outcomes before parsing the representation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"
